name: Orihuela del Tremedal – circular via the Camino de los Griegos (SL-TE 43)
name_original: Orihuela del Tremedal – circular por el Camino de los Griegos (SL-TE 43)
region_intro: Hiking around Orihuela Del Tremedal offers diverse landscapes within the Sierra de Albarracín, part of the Montes Universales. The region features mountainous terrain and dense forests, primarily black pine and Scots pine, contributing to its high-altitude vistas. Unique natural features include "tremedales" (peat bogs) and "rivers of stone," which are scree slopes formed by ice action on quartzite peaks. The River Gallo also originates in this area, adding to the varied natural environment. Best hiking trails around Orihuela Del Tremedal The most popular hiking route is Orihuela del Tremedal – circular via the Camino de los Griegos (SL-TE 43), a 6.0 miles (9.6 km) trail that takes 3 hours 13 minutes to complete. This circular path explores the local landscape, including sections of the notable Camino de los Griegos. Another top favourite among local hikers is Our Lady of Tremedal Trail, an easy 3.7 miles (5.9 km) path. This route leads towards the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Tremedal, offering a tranquil experience through the local terrain. Local hikers also love the Caimodorro loop from Collado de Juan Liria, a 6.1 miles (9.9 km) trail leading through mountainous forests, often completed in about 3 hours 9 minutes. Hiking around Orihuela Del Tremedal is defined by dense pine forests, unique peat bogs, and impressive "rivers of stone" formations, with high-altitude viewpoints. The network offers options for various ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging ascents. The routes in Orihuela Del Tremedal are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. More than 300 hikers have used komoot to explore Orihuela Del Tremedal's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Orihuela Del Tremedal?
A: There are over 15 hiking routes documented around Orihuela Del Tremedal, offering a variety of experiences for different skill levels. These include easy walks, moderate treks, and some more challenging ascents.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes in Orihuela Del Tremedal?
A: The terrain around Orihuela Del Tremedal is diverse, featuring mountainous landscapes and dense forests, primarily black and Scots pine. You'll encounter unique natural features such as 'tremedales' (peat bogs) with spongy soil and impressive 'rivers of stone,' which are scree slopes formed by ice action on quartzite peaks. The area also includes the origin of the River Gallo.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Orihuela Del Tremedal?
A: Due to its high altitude, Orihuela Del Tremedal offers pleasant conditions for hiking during spring, summer, and early autumn. Summers are generally mild, while spring and autumn provide vibrant colors and comfortable temperatures. Winters can be cold with snow, making some trails more challenging.

Q: Are there easy hiking trails suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy trails perfect for beginners or families. An excellent option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976217965/our-lady-of-tremedal-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Our Lady of Tremedal Trail&lt;/a&gt;, an easy 3.7-mile (5.9 km) path that leads towards the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Tremedal, offering a tranquil experience through the local terrain.

Q: Are there challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: For those seeking a more challenging experience, the region offers routes with significant elevation changes. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/23751909" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Majada de las Vacas Fountain – Orihuela del Tremedal loop from Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 17.1 km (10.6 miles) route with substantial ascent, providing a demanding trek through the mountainous landscape.

Q: What unique natural features can I see while hiking in Orihuela Del Tremedal?
A: Hikers can discover several unique natural features. The 'tremedales' or peat bogs are distinctive damp areas with specialized ecosystems. You'll also encounter 'rivers of stone,' which are striking scree slopes. The region is rich in dense pine forests, and the River Gallo originates here, adding to the natural beauty.

Q: Are there any viewpoints offering panoramic scenery?
A: Yes, the area boasts several spots with spectacular views. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portera Refuge and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; offers extraordinary vistas of the surrounding mountains. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3620455" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra Alta Summit&lt;/a&gt; and Pico San Cristobal are known for their panoramic rewards.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes in Orihuela Del Tremedal?
A: Yes, circular routes are a highlight of the area. A popular choice is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e976214801/orihuela-del-tremedal-circular-via-the-camino-de-los-griegos-sl-te-43" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal – circular via the Camino de los Griegos (SL-TE 43)&lt;/a&gt;, a 9.6 km (6.0 miles) path exploring the local landscape. The 'Ruta Circular Al Cañon' in the Sierra Alta area also offers remarkable rock formations.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about hiking in Orihuela Del Tremedal?
A: The area is highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 30 reviews. Reviewers often praise the diverse landscapes, the unique natural features like peat bogs and 'rivers of stone,' and the extensive network of trails through dense pine forests.

Q: Can I find long-distance hiking trails in the region?
A: Yes, for long-distance enthusiasts, sections of the GR 10 and GR 160 (Camino del Cid) footpaths pass through or originate from Orihuela Del Tremedal, offering opportunities for extended treks.

Q: Are there any interesting places or landmarks to explore near the trails?
A: Beyond the trails, you can explore the charming village of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2530405" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Orihuela del Tremedal&lt;/a&gt; itself. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3924939" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;La Portera Refuge and Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; is a notable spot, and the Sanctuary of the Virgin of Tremedal is a significant local landmark accessible via a dedicated trail.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter while hiking?
A: The extensive forest areas around Orihuela Del Tremedal offer excellent opportunities for bird watching. The La Toba Reservoir, for instance, serves as a stopover for grey herons. The diverse natural environment supports various species, making it a rewarding area for nature observation.
tips: The most interesting archaeological site is the Iberian settlement of Castillejo-El Toril, with the remains of a huge wall.
A great hermitage, in which I never accessed its interior, but to take a walk around its surroundings is to enjoy fantastic views.

In the southern area of the Youth Camp recite we find the Majada de las Vacas fountain and behind this fountain the start of the ascent of the Sanctuary path that will make us ascend to the hermitage of Tremedal or Alto del Tremedal.
The watering hole is continuously supplied with water and the youth camp site is an area of tables and paelleros, we are a recreational area but of a considerable size.
Orihuela del Tremedal has its roots in medieval times. It is believed that the town was founded in the 12th century, during the Reconquista, when Christians recovered territories from the Muslims.
